availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

part.delta: I'll create two child sessions to delegate triage work
, then observe the coordination.work channel for the inbound incidents.

params.prompt: You are Triage-Analyst-A for a webhook burst triage experiment. Your assignment: analyze Linear incidents (workIds matching linear:*). For each Linear incident you are told about, identify the external entity (e.g. TF-101, TF-102), classify severity (P0/P1/P2), and flag duplicates (same entity = duplicate group). Reply concisely in one message with a JSON-ish summary like {"entity":"TF-101","severity":"P1","duplicates":["linear:TF-101:a","linear:TF-101:duplicate"]}. Wait for my next message with the incident data before responding.

rawInput.prompt: You are Triage-Analyst-A for a webhook burst triage experiment. Your assignment: analyze Linear incidents (workIds matching linear:*). For each Linear incident you are told about, identify the external entity (e.g. TF-101, TF-102), classify severity (P0/P1/P2), and flag duplicates (same entity = duplicate group). Reply concisely in one message with a JSON-ish summary like {"entity":"TF-101","severity":"P1","duplicates":["linear:TF-101:a","linear:TF-101:duplicate"]}. Wait for my next message with the incident data before responding.

params.prompt: You are Triage-Analyst-B for a webhook burst triage experiment. Your assignment: analyze GitHub incidents (workIds matching github:*). For each GitHub incident you are told about, identify the failing check/entity, classify severity (P0/P1/P2), and flag duplicates. Reply concisely in one message with a JSON-ish summary like {"entity":"check:failed","severity":"P0","duplicates":["github:check:failed"]}. Wait for my next message with the incident data before responding.

rawInput.prompt: You are Triage-Analyst-B for a webhook burst triage experiment. Your assignment: analyze GitHub incidents (workIds matching github:*). For each GitHub incident you are told about, identify the failing check/entity, classify severity (P0/P1/P2), and flag duplicates. Reply concisely in one message with a JSON-ish summary like {"entity":"check:failed","severity":"P0","duplicates":["github:check:failed"]}. Wait for my next message with the incident data before responding.

part.delta: All four incidents received. Dispatching to children now.

params.prompt:
Triage these 3 Linear incidents and reply with grouped JSON (entity, severity, duplicates, evidence rowIds):
1) workId=linear:TF-101:a, title="linear.issue.updated TF-101", body="Issue TF-101 changed labels from bug to regression."
2) workId=linear:TF-102:a, title="linear.issue.updated TF-102", body="Issue TF-102 received a customer escalation comment."
3) workId=linear:TF-101:duplicate, title="linear.issue.updated TF-101 duplicate", body="Duplicate delivery for TF-101 with the same external event id."

Reply in ONE short message with deduped groups by external entity (TF-101, TF-102), severity, and which workIds map to each group.

rawInput.prompt:
Triage these 3 Linear incidents and reply with grouped JSON (entity, severity, duplicates, evidence rowIds):
1) workId=linear:TF-101:a, title="linear.issue.updated TF-101", body="Issue TF-101 changed labels from bug to regression."
2) workId=linear:TF-102:a, title="linear.issue.updated TF-102", body="Issue TF-102 received a customer escalation comment."
3) workId=linear:TF-101:duplicate, title="linear.issue.updated TF-101 duplicate", body="Duplicate delivery for TF-101 with the same external event id."

Reply in ONE short message with deduped groups by external entity (TF-101, TF-102), severity, and which workIds map to each group.

params.prompt:
Triage this GitHub incident and reply with grouped JSON (entity, severity, duplicates, evidence):
1) workId=github:check:failed, title="github.check.failed", body="CI failed for a related branch; failure signature mentions unknown-channel."

Reply in ONE short message with the deduped group, severity, and any cross-entity correlation hint (e.g. failure signature mentions 'unknown-channel' — does this look related to a known issue class?).

rawInput.prompt:
Triage this GitHub incident and reply with grouped JSON (entity, severity, duplicates, evidence):
1) workId=github:check:failed, title="github.check.failed", body="CI failed for a related branch; failure signature mentions unknown-channel."

Reply in ONE short message with the deduped group, severity, and any cross-entity correlation hint (e.g. failure signature mentions 'unknown-channel' — does this look related to a known issue class?).

part.delta: Waiting for Analyst A's first event.
Got a TextChunk from Analyst A.
Collecting more of A's response and B's reply.
I have observed text replies from both children. Publishing the final artifact now.

params.payload:
{"kind": "final", "title": "Webhook burst triage — central orchestrator", "status": "complete", "runId": "2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z", "arm": "central", "scenarioId": "webhook-burst", "body": "Deduped incident groups (by external entity):

1) linear:TF-101 — severity P2 (label flip bug→regression). Evidence rows: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate (same external event id, deduped).
2) linear:TF-102 — severity P1 (customer escalation comment). Evidence rows: linear:TF-102:a.
3) github:check:failed — severity P1 (CI failure; signature mentions 'unknown-channel'). Evidence rows: github:check:failed. Correlation hint: 'unknown-channel' signature is a known channel-routing failure class but no direct linkage to TF-101/TF-102 from the evidence given.

Evidence inspected: 4 coordination.work rows above + child analyst replies.

Children created: Analyst-A (Linear lane) confirmed TF-101 dedup pair + TF-102 singleton; Analyst-B (GitHub lane) flagged check:failed with unknown-channel hint. Their replies shaped the dedup grouping and severities.

Recommendation: dedup webhook handlers by (source, external entity id) before triage; route P1 customer-escalation + CI failures to oncall; treat regression-label flips as P2 review.

Open questions: Is the 'unknown-channel' CI failure linked to either Linear ticket? Need a correlator over branch→ticket. Did orchestration help here? Marginally — only 1 of 4 work rows had ambiguity needing a specialist; lane split added overhead vs payoff."}

Evidence inspected: 4 coordination.work rows above + child analyst replies.

Children created: Analyst-A (Linear lane) confirmed TF-101 dedup pair + TF-102 singleton; Analyst-B (GitHub lane) flagged check:failed with unknown-channel hint. Their replies shaped the dedup grouping and severities.
